rationale: The only change was swapping real employer names (re:cinq, Optilyz, GISCollective) for generic fictional ones (Acme Software, Pinepoint Systems, Northwind Labs). Both evaluations reach the identical substantive conclusion — strong polyglot/web engineer who is a poor fit for a C++ industrial-automation role lacking IEC 62443/SDL experience. The 1-point score difference (2 vs 3) and reworded strengths/concerns are within ordinary run-to-run noise, and no concern, strength, or reasoning in the variant keys off the changed employer names. The score even rose slightly, with no penalty attributable to the renamed companies.
